api.api_views module¶

class api.api_views.ApprovedWebhookAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: APIView

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
post(request)¶
class api.api_views.ArticleListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

get_permissions()¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

perform_create(serializer)¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class api.api_views.ArticleRetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView(**kwargs)¶

get_permissions()¶

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

get_queryset()¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

perform_update(serializer)¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Article: New>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)¶

Save this form’s self.instance object if commit=True. Otherwise, add a save_m2m() method to the form which can be called after the instance is saved manually at a later time. Return the model instance.

class api.forms.SubscriptionForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, field_order=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None, bound_field_class=None)¶

Bases: Form

Form for subscriptions to publishers and journalists

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

api.models module¶

class api.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: Model

Displays article qulities and if approved or not

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶

api.tests module¶

Bases: TestCase

setUp()¶

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_auth_required()¶

test_editor_can_delete_article()¶
test_journalist_can_create_article()¶
test_newsletter_creation()¶

api.urls module¶

api.views module¶

api.views.approval_queue(request)¶

Displays approval queue for editors

api.views.approve_article(request, pk)¶

Allows editor to approve articles

api.views.article_delete(request, pk)¶

Allows editors and journalists to delete articles

api.views.article_detail(request, pk)¶

Displays article detail per role

api.views.article_edit(request, pk)¶

Allows editors and journalists to edit articles

api.views.article_list(request)¶

Displays list of articles per role.

api.views.create_article(request)¶

Allows journalists and editors to create articles

api.views.dashboard(request)¶

Displays dashboard according to user.

api.views.editor_dashboard(request)¶

Displays editor dashboard Editors can apprve articles and view arrpoval queue Editors can edit, create, delete articles and newsletters

api.views.home(request)¶

Home page of application

api.views.journalist_dashboard(request)¶

Displays journalist dashboard Journalists can create, edit, delete articles and newsletters

api.views.login_view(request)¶

Allows registered users to login

api.views.logout_view(request)¶

” Allows logged in users to logout

api.views.manage_subscriptions(request)¶

Allows reader to add, edit and view subscriptions

api.views.newsletter_create(request)¶

Allows journalists and editors to create newsletters

api.views.newsletter_delete(request, pk)¶

Allows journalists and editors to delete newsletters

api.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)¶

Newsletter details display

api.views.newsletter_edit(request, pk)¶

Allows journalists and editors to edit newsletters

api.views.newsletter_list(request)¶

Displays list of newsletters

api.views.publisher_dashboard(request)¶

Displays publisher dashboard Publishers can view articles and news letters under them

api.views.reader_dashboard(request)¶

Dashboard for reader only Readers can view subscription articles and newsletters Readers can subscribe to publishers and journalists

api.views.register_view(request)¶

Allows new users to register. Users can choose their roles.

api.views.view_articles(request)¶

Displays view of articles

Module contents¶

vault-times
